title: Add User model with password hash field
description: Add a passwordHash field to the Users collection for JWT authentication. This involves adding a passwordHash field with secure hashing (bcrypt or argon2), ensuring it's never exposed in API responses via field-level access controls, and implementing email uniqueness validation. The existing Users collection already has Payload's built-in auth with hash/salt fields, so this adds a custom passwordHash that coexists alongside Payload's built-in auth.
existing_patterns: src/collections/Users.ts - Payload collection config with auth enabled, field-level access controls, hidden fields (refreshToken, tokenExpiresAt)
src/auth/auth-service.ts - verifyPassword using PBKDF2, AuthService class with dependency injection pattern
src/auth/user-store.ts - hashPassword/verifyPassword utilities, User interface with passwordHash field
questions: Which secure algorithm should be used - bcrypt or argon2?
Should the new passwordHash field replace Payload's built-in auth hash, or coexist alongside it?
What is the maximum password length limit for validation?
